exemplar.title:
To what extent has the spread of the COVID-19 virus influenced the
economy of the households in Dar-es-salaam, Tanzania?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent has the spread of the COVID-19 virus influenced the
economy of the households in Dar-es-salaam, Tanzania?

strengths: The topic is communicated clearly with explicit geographic scale, time frame and economic focus throughout.
The research question is precisely stated and consistently reinforced, signifying evaluative intent.
The mixed-methods design is coherent and interdisciplinary, with ethical considerations and limitations explicitly addressed.
weaknesses: The justification for purposive sampling could include more detail on participant selection rationale.
Operational details of data-collection tools and statistical treatment remain under-developed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although minor operational details in the methodology warrant further depth, the clarity of topic definition, the tightly focused research question and the well-argued method selection align fully with the top-band descriptors for Focus and Method, justifying the maximum mark.

strengths: Source material is clearly relevant and expertly chosenâacademic articles, World Bank and WHO reports are integrated effectively.
Demonstrates coherent, integrated knowledge across biological and economic domains.
Terminology and concepts (e.g., ACE2 receptor, aggregate demand shifts) are accurate, consistent and employed with depth.
weaknesses: A more critical evaluation of source reliability in the introductory global-impact claims would strengthen scholarly rigor.
Some popular website sources could be replaced with additional peer-reviewed studies for greater authority.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection and use of terminology, coherent integration of multi-disciplinary knowledge and relevance of sources meet the highest band. Minor critiques of source evaluation do not detract from overall expert performance in Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Research draws on rigorously selected primary and secondary data directly relevant to the investigation.
Analysis is incisive, linking empirical findings to theoretical constructs with well-supported conclusions.
Argument is logical, coherently structured and demonstrates nuanced interdisciplinary evaluation.
weaknesses: The quantitative depthâsuch as precise elasticity estimates or statistical measuresâcould be expanded.
Critical engagement with the limitations of key secondary studies is relatively brief.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite minor gaps in quantitative elaboration and source-evaluation depth, the investigation consistently displays rigorous source selection, incisive analysis and a sophisticated, coherent argument, satisfying all top-band descriptors for Critical Thinking.

strengths: Exemplary academic structure with clear headings, a full table of contents and logical flow.
Visual elements (charts, diagrams, maps) are well chosen and integrated effectively.
Referencing and bibliography are complete and consistently formatted.
weaknesses: A few figure captions lack full source details or numbering.
Minor inconsistencies in in-text citation style remain.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report fully meets the highest standards for Presentation: academic conventions are observed throughout, visuals are appropriate and referencing is consistently applied, meriting the maximum mark.

strengths: Explicit ethical considerations (informed consent, anonymization) demonstrate some reflective awareness in methodology.
weaknesses: No RPPF or standalone reflective statements are provided, preventing any formal assessment of reflections, personal engagement or intellectual initiative.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: In the absence of an RPPF or dedicated reflective content, no evidence is available to award marks in Engagement, resulting in a score of zero as per IB rubric guidance.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical progression (introduction â literature â data â conclusion) is maintained and some interdisciplinary links are made, but evaluation of method and sources is brief and the argument occasionally slips into description.  Hence âstructured argument with some critical insightâ fits best (mid band, lower end).

A complete bibliography is provided and in-text citations appear, yet the formatting oscillates between APA-like and informal styles and a few citations are incomplete.  Consistency is partial, warranting the middle score in the 1-2-3 range.

The student states a precise local topic â the economic impact of COVID-19 on households in Dar-es-Salaam â in the title, introduction and throughout the essay.  The geographic scale, time frame and economic dimension are all explicitly signposted, leaving little ambiguity about the studyâs focus.  The clarity and appropriateness of that communication align with the top-band description.
The essay contains a title page, table of contents, logical section headings and coherent flow, satisfying IB academic structure conventions.

The essay demonstrates clear descriptive knowledge of viral structure and elementary economic theory, and attempts to apply these to Dar-es-Salaam.  Integration, depth and quantitative treatment are limited, so knowledge is present but only âused with some effectiveness,â matching the mid band.
No RPPF or separate reflective statements are supplied, so there is no evidence on which to judge reflection quality.

The student compares primary and secondary findings, draws reasoned statements about income and expenditure, and links them to theory.  Conclusions are generally supported, yet statistical depth and counter-argument are thin, preventing the jump to the 7-9 band.

The research question is quoted verbatim on the title page and again in the introduction.  It is sufficiently narrow (one city, household level, specific pandemic) to be investigable and is framed by the phrase âto what extent,â signalling an evaluative intent.  Minor breadth remains (many different economic variables) so the focus is very good rather than perfect, justifying a 5 rather than 6.

Most secondary sources are credible and clearly linked to aspects of the investigation, and the primary data â while small â are directly relevant.  This places the work solidly in the 4-6 band but not at the 7-9 level, where rigorous selection and comprehensive coverage would be expected.
Without the RPPF there is no documented evidence of intellectual initiative such as proposing future research directions; therefore this strand must be awarded zero.

The mixed-methods design (online survey, purposive interviews, secondary literature) is sensible and ethics/limitations are acknowledged, showing informed selection.  However, the primary sample is only 20 respondents and the justification for purposive sampling remains brief.  Data-collection tools, statistical treatment and interdisciplinary linkages are not described in enough operational detail for the highest band, so band 3-4 fits best.

Academic articles, World Bank reports and WHO pages are used and generally relevant to biology and economics; nevertheless, the range is modest and some popular web sources are relied upon.  Selection is therefore âmostly relevant and appropriateâ rather than expert.
Because the engagement criterion relies exclusively on the RPPF, and none is provided, no personal engagement can be credited.

Charts, supply-and-demand diagrams and maps are integrated, but some images lack proper figure numbers or full source captions, and resolution is low.  This meets the basic requirement but not the clarity expected for the top band.

Key biological (ACE2, TMPRSS2) and economic (aggregate demand, price elasticity) terms are employed correctly and consistently, with only minor lapses.  The precision of terminology meets the standard for the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The final paragraph succinctly restates the research question and outlines specific economic variables, effectively clarifying the essayâs focus.
Ethical considerations are explicitly addressed, with informed consent and anonymization steps clearly outlined.
The ethical section would be stronger with more detail on interview protocols and sampling rationale to demonstrate depth of method justification.
The literature review effectively summarizes Ipagala et al. (2023), but remains descriptive. Engage critically by evaluating methodology or limitations of that study.
The title clearly signals the investigationâs focus on COVID-19âs economic impact, but narrowing to Dar-es-Salaam and specifying the timeframe would sharpen scope and focus.
Word count is clearly stated and remains under the 4000-word limit, satisfying IB formatting requirements.
The Table of Contents lists all major sections, ensuring a coherent overview of the essayâs structure.
Headings in the contents lack page numbers; adding the corresponding page references will improve navigability and presentation.
Introductory context is well established, but the opening claims about COVID-19âs global impact would benefit from a more critical evaluation of source reliability.
The justification for choosing Dar-es-Salaam as a case study is well argued, demonstrating clear interdisciplinary method selection.
The sentence ending âThus, thisâ is incomplete, interrupting the argumentâs flow. Ensure each paragraph concludes fully to maintain coherence.
The description of viral structure uses correct terminology (S, N, M, E proteins), demonstrating strong subject knowledge.
The figure on page 8 lacks a clear figure number and complete source citation in caption, reducing presentation clarity.
The global economy section transitions logically and sets context before focusing on Tanzania, demonstrating clear methodological planning.
The paragraph on postponed healthcare services remains descriptive. Explicitly link these service disruptions to household economic burdens to strengthen analysis.
The AD shift diagram is well chosen, illustrating macroeconomic effects of COVID-19 in Tanzania clearly.
The supply-demand diagram is correctly labelled, showing a clear shift from Sâ to Sâ and its price effects, demonstrating accurate use of economic concepts.
The statement on inelastic demand lacks quantitative support. Incorporating specific elasticity estimates would reinforce the analysis.
Citation for the WIEGO report is inconsistently formatted; align this in-text citation with bibliography style for consistency.
Discussion of human-to-human transmission is thorough and supported by reputable sources, reflecting solid understanding.
The ACE2 receptor description could cite more precise studies or numerical data on receptor affinity to deepen the biological analysis.
The caption explaining the shift to APLâ and Yâ is cut off; expand the text to detail how real output changes and its significance.
The methodology section identifies purposive sampling but lacks detailed justification of participant selection and sample size rationale.
Inclusion of a map showing the data collection site effectively grounds the methodology in a real context.
The limitations section insightfully acknowledges recall bias, demonstrating critical awareness of primary data constraints.
The mitigation strategy suggests using secondary data, but referencing specific reports or datasets would clarify how bias is reduced.
The introduction to data presentation succinctly ties primary and secondary data back to the research question.
The small sample size (20 respondents) limits generalizability; discuss how this may affect the reliability and validity of your conclusions.
The research question is consistently stated on the title page and introduction, reinforcing focus and coherence.
The hierarchy in the Table of Contents would benefit from consistent indentation of subsections to enhance readability.
The inclusion of the bibliography entry in the contents demonstrates attention to academic structure conventions.
